Privacy Policy (GDPR / RGPD)

This Privacy Policy explains how Anjo Cleaning Unipessoal Lda processes personal data in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and applicable Portuguese law.

1. Controller

Anjo Cleaning Unipessoal Lda
Campus da Penteada â€“ Startup Madeira EV 2789
9020-105 SÃ£o Roque, Funchal, Madeira, Portugal

Represented by: Florian Engelmann
Email: info@anjo-cleaning.com
Phone: +351 926 669 223

2. Data Protection Contact

For privacy enquiries, please contact: info@anjo-cleaning.com.

3. Hosting (STRATO)

This website is hosted by STRATO AG, Otto-Ostrowski-StraÃŸe 7, 10249 Berlin, Germany. STRATO processes personal data (e.g., IP address) as a processor for hosting purposes. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in secure and stable operation). A data processing agreement (Art. 28 GDPR) is in place.

4. Server logs

  • Data processed: IP address, date/time, requested URL, referrer, user agent, status code, transferred data volume.
  • Purpose: ensuring technical stability and security (e.g., attack detection, troubleshooting).
  • Retention: typically short-term; logs are deleted or anonymised as soon as no longer required for the purpose (hosting/security).

5. Google Tag Manager (GTM)

We use Google Tag Manager (Google Ireland Limited, Dublin, Ireland) to manage website tags. GTM itself typically does not set analytics cookies, but it can trigger other services. Non-essential measurement tags are activated only after consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).

6. Third-country transfers (e.g., USA)

When using Google services, transfers of personal data to third countries (especially the USA) cannot be ruled out. Where required, transfers are based on appropriate safeguards (e.g., EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework or Standard Contractual Clauses under Art. 46 GDPR).

7. Purposes

  • Responding to enquiries and preparing quotations
  • Contract performance and service delivery
  • Customer communication and scheduling
  • Fulfilment of legal obligations (tax and accounting)

8. Legal Basis (Art. 6 GDPR)

  • Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR â€“ contract / pre-contractual measures
  • Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR â€“ legal obligation
  • Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR â€“ legitimate interest
  • Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR â€“ consent (where applicable)

9. Retention Periods

We retain personal data only as long as necessary:

  • Enquiries without contract: up to 12 months.
  • Contract, invoicing & accounting: typically up to 10 years (Portuguese retention obligations may apply).
  • Legal claims: according to applicable limitation periods.

10. Your Rights

  • Access (Art. 15 GDPR)
  • Rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)
  • Erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)
  • Restriction (Art. 18 GDPR)
  • Data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
  • Object (Art. 21 GDPR)
  • Withdraw consent at any time (where applicable)

11. Supervisory Authority

CNPD â€“ ComissÃ£o Nacional de ProteÃ§Ã£o de Dados

Phone: +351 213 928 400
Website: www.cnpd.pt

12. Storage

This website stores your cookie consent choice (accept/decline) locally so the banner does not reappear on every visit. Non-essential tracking tags (if used) are activated only after consent.

Last updated: 2026-02-14

We use cookies to operate the website reliably and improve usage. You can accept or decline. Details are in the privacy policy. Privacy
